translated: This is the time for us to come together and support each other. Let us work together to overcome our challenges and build a better future for our children and grandchildren. Let us not forget our past and learn from it.

text: Bwe maa kpɔa pa dɛ kpɔ dɛɛ liiri nɔɔ dɛɛ ba he torome bɔr ɛɛ. Ásɛ komɛlbwa ɡe haa tɛ mɛ la, bɔre fi bɛlbwa la, mɛɛ tɛɛ bɔrbeŋkpoŋii.”
translated: When you are ready to go, go quickly and do not linger.  Let the work be done quickly, let the work be done well, let the work be done with care.

ref: The Lord God took some soil from the ground and made a man. God breathed life into the man, and the man started breathing.

translated: The pot was broken. It was not good. It was not good to eat from it. They said that Joseph had broken it.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.

text: Asɛ dɛ jaamo dɛ ŋɔ, “Korowiirii kpɔ he naa la. Dɛɛ ba! Ɛrɛɛ he ba che dahara ɡɛ nɔɔ? Dea haa chɔ ne bela he nyoɡmare nɔ aa chɔ bwa lole.”
translated: If you ask me what I am, I will say, “I am Korowiirii, the one who is always there. You see! I am the one who will always be there for you, no matter what. I will always be there to help you, no matter what happens.

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Anomanta, ɡaaɡe harɛ la bwa ta, dɛ́ na ɡɛ waa doa dɛkalkɛŋɔ he nɔn taa mɛɛ ba kpɔe do.”
translated: Anomanta, the one who is always talking, he is the one who will bring the wood, he will not let the fire go out.
